text: CEASEFIRE PLAN: US delivers 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran via Pakistan — covers sanctions relief, nuclear rollback, IAEA monitoring, Hormuz access; proposes 1-month ceasefire; Iran military mocks US: 'negotiating with yourselves'

sources.name: Bloomberg — US drafts 15-point plan to end Iran war
Time — Trump peace proposal via Pakistan

text: SUBMARINE CENTER DESTROYED: IDF destroys Iran's sole submarine R&D facility in Isfahan + two cruise missile production sites in Tehran — Israeli naval intelligence guided strikes; Iran's entire submarine development capability eliminated

sources.name: Times of Israel — Day 26 liveblog: submarine facility destroyed
CBS News — Iran war live updates March 25

text: BUSHEHR WORST-CASE: Rosatom chief declares Bushehr nuclear plant situation 'worst-case scenario' after 2nd strike — begins 3rd evacuation phase; ~480 Russian staff remain; IAEA urges 'maximum restraint'

sources.name: Al Arabiya — Rosatom worst-case scenario at Bushehr
CGTN — IAEA urges maximum restraint after Bushehr attack

text: POWER PLANT TARGETED: Iran ballistic missile strikes near Orot Rabin — Israel's largest power plant (2,590 MW, ~20% of national supply) in Hadera; IEC confirms no damage or casualties

sources.name: Times of Israel — IEC says no damage after missile near Hadera power station

text: LEBANON OCCUPIED: Israel formally announces occupation of south Lebanon up to Litani River (~30km) — 'security zone' covers ~10% of country; Smotrich calls for annexation; Hezbollah declares 'existential threat'; airstrikes kill 9

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — Israel announces territorial seizure to Litani
France 24 — Israeli strikes on south Lebanon leave nine dead

text: 82ND AIRBORNE: Pentagon confirms deployment orders for 2,000-3,000 paratroopers + 2 Marine Expeditionary Units — possible Kharg Island seizure mission (90% of Iran's oil exports); 6,000-8,000 total troops en route

sources.name: NPR — Pentagon orders 82nd Airborne to Middle East
Stars and Stripes — 82nd Airborne ordered to deploy

text: SAUDI DEFENSE: Saudi Arabia intercepts 32+ Iranian drones + 1 ballistic missile over 11 hours — targeting Ras Tanura, Ghawar, Abqaiq oil facilities; largest single-day drone wave at Saudi Arabia

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Day 26 liveblog: Saudi intercepts drone wave
PBS — Strikes hit Iran while Tehran targets Gulf states

text: TEHRAN STRUCK: Strike on southern Tehran residential area kills 12, wounds 28; school and buildings destroyed in east Tehran; Hengaw 7th report: 6,530 killed in Iran (640 civilians)

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Day 26 report: Tehran residential strike
Hengaw — 7th report: 6,530 killed in Iran

text: OIL PLUNGES: Brent crude drops ~6% to ~$99/bbl on ceasefire plan hopes — first time below $100 since Day 22; WTI falls to ~$88.50; $580M in bets on falling oil placed 15 min before Trump announcement

sources.name: Fortune — Oil prices today March 25
CNBC — Oil prices today WTI Brent

text: RESERVISTS EXPANDED: Israel authorizes IDF to draft up to 400,000 reservists — Knesset extends state of emergency to April 14; military estimates several more weeks to complete war goals

sources.name: Times of Israel — Day 26 liveblog: 400,000 reservists
Al Jazeera — Day 26 updates

text: SENATE VOTE FAILS: War powers resolution fails 47-53 for 3rd time — 59% of Americans say military action excessive (AP-NORC); Trump approval at record-low 36%

sources.name: NBC News — Iran war live updates: Senate war powers vote fails

text: IRAQ CLINIC STRUCK: Airstrike hits Habbaniyah Military Clinic — 7 killed, 13 wounded; ~50 miles from Baghdad; continued escalation of Iraq theater

sources.name: CBS News — Iran war live updates: Habbaniyah clinic struck

text: JAPAN OIL RESERVES: Japan announces largest-ever strategic oil reserve release — 30 days starting Thursday; part of IEA 400M barrel coordinated release from 32 nations

sources.name: NPR — IEA oil reserves release

text: IRAN CRACKDOWN: 50 arrested in Mazandaran for sharing military info with diaspora media; 68 nationwide for ties to monarchists/MEK; Pope Leo XIV renews ceasefire call for 12th time

sources.name: NCRI — Iran news in brief March 25
Crux — Pope Leo XIV renews ceasefire call

text: DESALINATION THREAT: UN official warns Iran may target Gulf desalination plants 'within days' — would affect 100M+ people's water supply; Australia bans Iranian tourist visas for 6 months

sources.name: Fox News — Iran threatens mass water war, UN official warns

text: IRAN COUNTER-DEMANDS: Iran demands closure of all US Gulf bases, war reparations, Hormuz transit fees, missile program retention, full sanctions lift — US official calls demands 'ridiculous and unrealistic'

sources.name: Fox News — Iran raises stakes of ceasefire talks
Jerusalem Post — Iran counter-demands

text: WAVE 80 BARRAGE: IRGC fires 4 successive missile salvos at Tel Aviv metro area in 40 minutes — Kheibar Shekan, Emad, Sejjil ballistic missiles + drones; also strikes US bases in Kuwait (Ali al-Salem, Arifjan), Jordan (al-Azraq), Bahrain (Sheikh Isa); several interceptions over Tel Aviv, Hadera, Bnei Brak; debris causes vehicle fire

sources.name: PressTV — IRGC announces Wave 80 operation
Al Jazeera — Day 26 live: four successive salvos hit Tel Aviv metro

text: IRAN REJECTS PEACE PLAN: Iran formally rejects US 15-point plan — issues 5 counter-conditions: halt aggression, guarantee no resumption, pay war reparations, end Lebanon/Iraq proxy attacks, international recognition of Iran's authority over Strait of Hormuz; Araghchi: 'No negotiations until then'

sources.name: Press TV — Iran rejects US proposal, lays out 5 conditions
NPR — Iran rejects Trump's proposal to end the war

text: HEZBOLLAH COMMANDER KILLED: Israeli drone kills Hassan Kamal Halawi — commander of Hezbollah's anti-tank missile unit — in strike on vehicle in Qaaqaait al-Jisr, southern Lebanon; 5th Litani River bridge destroyed in separate airstrike severing Nabatieh-Wadi al-Hujeir route

sources.name: Naharnet — Israeli drone targets vehicle in Qaaqaait al-Jisr
Al Jazeera — Day 26: Hezbollah commander killed

text: FIRST EVER INTERCEPT: Iranian ballistic missile intercepted over Lebanon for first time — IDF describes event as unprecedented; IAF opens investigation into two separate air defense failures (Dimona + Arad); David's Sling gaps probed; IAF finds no link between the two incidents

sources.name: Times of Israel — Day 26 liveblog: first intercept over Lebanon
Washington Post — air defense failures under investigation

text: PHILIPPINES ENERGY EMERGENCY: President Marcos declares national State of Energy Emergency — first country globally to do so; 98% of oil imported from Gulf; fuel reserves: gasoline 53 days, diesel 46 days, LPG 23.5 days; 4-day government work week ordered; Asia-wide austerity spreading (Japan, South Korea)

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Philippine president declares energy emergency
Newsweek — US ally declares national emergency over Iran war

text: GULF WAR ENTRY: Saudi Arabia and UAE moving closer to joining war on US-Israel side per WSJ — Saudi Arabia already allowing use of military bases, marking major policy shift; Iran threatens to seize Bahraini and UAE coastlines if US 'makes any mistake'; Iran names Zolghadr as new SNSC secretary replacing slain Larijani

sources.name: Wall Street Journal via Alma Research — Saudi, UAE war entry
Times of Israel — Iran threatens Bahrain, UAE coastlines

text: CENTCOM MILESTONE: US strikes 10,000th Iranian target — 92% of Iran's largest naval vessels destroyed, missile/drone attack pace down 90% since Day 1

VINnews — B-52 bombers delivering 70,000 lbs of munitions per mission

text: KUWAIT AIRPORT HIT: Iranian drones strike fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport — fire erupts, no casualties; 6 Hezbollah plotters arrested in Kuwait

text: GULF WAR ENTRY IMMINENT: Saudi Arabia grants US access to King Fahd Air Base; UAE discussing troop deployment — both nations moving toward joining US-Israel coalition against Iran
